name: EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) to SicarioSpec Evidence Map

framework.name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence (Artificial Intelligence Act)

scope_note: Risk-tier and high-risk-obligation-level starter mapping only. The EU AI Act classifies systems into prohibited (Art. 5), high-risk (Art. 6 / Annex III), limited-risk transparency (Art. 50), and minimal-risk tiers, and sets provider obligations for high-risk systems in Articles 9-15. This map links SicarioSpec governance evidence to the high-risk obligations. It is legal-/regulatory-shaped guidance, not legal advice or a conformity assessment. Confirm classification, applicability, and conformity with qualified counsel and a notified body where required.

risk_tier_notes.note: Unacceptable-risk practices are banned outright; SicarioSpec evidence cannot make a prohibited use compliant.
Subject to the Article 9-15 provider obligations mapped below, plus quality management, registration, and conformity assessment.
Transparency duties (e.g. disclose AI interaction, label synthetic content).
No mandatory obligations; voluntary codes of conduct encouraged.

high_risk_obligation_mappings.intent: Establish, document, and maintain an iterative risk management process across the AI lifecycle, including foreseeable misuse.
Govern training, validation, and test data for relevance, representativeness, error rates, and bias.
Maintain documentation describing the system, its design, capabilities, limitations, and evaluation before placing on the market.
Enable automatic event logging over the system lifetime to support traceability and post-market monitoring.

high_risk_obligation_mappings.obligation: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
high_risk_obligation_mappings.intent: Provide instructions for use and design the system so deployers can interpret and use its output appropriately.
Design the system to be effectively overseen by humans, including stop/override and human approval gates.
Maintain appropriate accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, including resilience to errors, misuse, and adversarial attacks (e.g. prompt injection).
